title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
focus: Couples a seq2seq generator with dense retrieval so external documents act as non-parametric memory.
summary: Strong baseline for retrieval-augmented generation on knowledge-intensive tasks, with clear separation between retrieved evidence and generation.
limitations: Uses a comparatively simple retrieval stack and predates later self-correction and retrieval-quality control methods.

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
focus: Adds reflection tokens so the model can decide when to retrieve and critique its own responses.
summary: Better fit when the system must adapt retrieval behavior per question and justify its answers with self-critique.
limitations: Training and runtime behavior are more complex than a standard retrieve-then-generate pipeline.

title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
focus: Evaluates retrieval quality, corrects weak retrieval with fallback web knowledge, and refines evidence before generation.
summary: Useful when retrieval quality is inconsistent and the application needs an explicit quality-control step before generation.
limitations: Adds corrective logic and external-search assumptions that increase system complexity for a small POC.
